etymology.description: Depicts a hand 又(又) (yòu, "hand") holding a tool, stylized as 卜(卜) (bǔ, "divination") -> meaning "to hit"
etymology.interpretation: 攵 is a stylized pictogram originally depicting a hand holding a tool or weapon, representing action or hitting. The hand component evolved into a more uniform stylistic form using 卜(卜) while preserving the core meaning.

examples.chinese: 他埋怨我的方式(他埋怨我的方式)总是冲动而不经过思考(總是衝動而不經過思考)。
examples.pinyin: Tā mányuàn wǒ de fāngshì zǒng shì chōngdòng ér bù jīngguò sīkǎo.
examples.english: He complains that my actions are always impulsive and thoughtless.

examples.chinese: 需要的不是空谈(需要的不是空談)，而是切实的行动(而是切實的行動)。
examples.pinyin: Xūyào de bù shì kōngtán, ér shì qiēshí de xíngdòng.
examples.english: What is needed is not empty talk, but concrete action.
